desc:
The road runs to the north and the west through barren fields that stretch out
in all directions, save for the sharp spur that looms over the road to the
northwest. The fields are covered with sparse patches of brown, dry grass, and
the occasional small, prickly bush. The road, however, is built on soft sand,
paved with large bricks and broken stones, allowing heavy wagons to travel. A
few clusters of small flowers grow by the side of the road and expand to the
south.
name: Ancient Broken Road

desc:
Curving from north to south, this large and dusty road cuts through rock walls
on the east and west sides. The cut that this road runs through does not allow
travel to the east or west. Small patches of grass grow beside the road, whilst
the road itself is paved with many bricks and stones, reinforcing it. Every here
and there a small thicket of thorn bushes can be spotted, growing at the side of
the road.
name: Ancient Broken Road

desc:
No water flows now, but it appears that at some time in the past a stream ran
here, carving this channel out of the living rock. Though the cliffs to the
south appear to offer no viable route between the plains and the mountain
slopes, this ancient water course cuts back into them, twisting and curving its
way between the valley and the heights above. The rock is fairly smooth, but it
should be possible to travel this way, albeit only with considerable effort.
name: Dry Channel

desc:
A narrow entrance leads down into a cave deep in the mountains. Moisture and
darkness grip the rocky walls. The filthy floor is somehow sticky to the touch
and reek of rot and decay fills the air. Large foot prints in the dirt suggest
that this is a Trollhome alongside the remnants of previous meals.
name: Trollhome

desc:
Something has left giant claw marks across the walls and ceiling of this cave as
it descends, clearly the result of some animal-like excavation. The rocky burrow
continues further down where the stench gets more foul. A filthy and perilous
crack leads out to the west, following a slight breeze.
name: Dark Cave

desc:
Bones and filth covers the ground of this smelly cave that has been excavated
out of the mountain. Giant claw marks can be seen running along the sides of the
walls of the cavern, seemingly in an attempt to expand it. The air is absolutely
foul here and its source seems to be some bedding in the corner.
name: Smelly Cavern

desc:
The valley lies to the west, sheltered by mountains to the south and hills to
the north and east. Long blades of dark green grass dance in the gentle wind,
creating waves that ripple across the vale. To the south, a tall cliff face
marks the starting point of a mountain spur, an offshoot of the Misty Mountains
that dominate the horizon far to the east.
name: Edge of a Lush Valley

desc:
Scattered trees dot the landscape among these rolling hills, growing denser to
the east, where they climb up the slopes of more hills. To the west and
northwest, the hills continue, their grassy slopes receding far into the
distance. As the slopes march down into the valley below, to the south, the
grass becomes much longer, and the vegetation lusher.
name: Grassy Hills

desc:
These plains are slightly raised, with some of the run-off from the nearby hill
and mountain ranges supplying much needed moisture. The vegetation is green and
broad-leaf bushes grow in abundance here. The bushes are hardy, being themselves
the only natural windbreak in a land where the prevailing winds are cruel.
name: Green Brush

desc:
Curling with the contours of the lands that lie to the southeast, a stream flows
here from the distant mountains, bringing its cold, clear water to the plains.
The banks to either side are alive with lush green grass and a proliferation of
low bushes. Further off to the west and south, a range of hills can be seen on
the horizon.
name: Meandering Stream

desc:
Lush, green grass can be seen growing along either bank of the stream, whose
waters flow from south to west here. The stream bed is rather more rocky here
than either upstream or downstream, and so the waters run much more rapidly,
dancing and bouncing off the rocks as they go. The water is very cold and
crystal clear, fed from run-offs from the mountain range to the east.
name: Deep Stream

desc:
A narrow tunnel, extending about fifteen feet from an opening in the ground,
leads onto an enlarged burrow. Dug into the red-brown soil, the walls, floor and
ceiling are earthen, and are reinforced naturally by a proliferation of tangled
roots. Though this is obviously a den of some sort, there is no sign of bones,
fur, or any kind of animal activity.
name: Warm Den

desc:
A deep cave navigates its way into the mountain before ending in a cave-in. A
strong musky smell permeates the area, as if some huge beast has marked its
territory over the years. A large boulder juts out in the centre of the lair,
providing cover for a massive hollow that is lined with bones and fur.
name: Dark, Musty Lair

desc:
The ground here is quite alive with grasses and occasional trees. Some rocky
ground is apparent to the north, and to the south the plant growth is noticeably
higher than here, probably maintained by a stream that flows idly in the
distance. To the west, the ground rises sharply at some grassy hills.
name: Fertile Fields

desc:
The stream that flows by to the west has lent its life-giving waters to this
part of the plains, making the lands lush and green. Large, broad-leaf bushes
compete with a sea of tall grass to define the landscape here. Far to the south
a range of hills cuts across the horizon, while to the east the Misty Mountains
dominate the vista.
name: Brush-covered Plains by a Stream

desc:
The ground is uneven here, filled with stones and boulders. The soil seems quite
fertile in the area, and there grows some grass and bushes here. Some of the
taller plants and small trees look trampled, cut, or torn, perhaps by some
animals. The fields continue to every direction.
name: Trampled Fields

desc:
The land is covered by short grasses which ripple when the wind blows through
it. There are grassy fields to the west from here and the large bank of a stream
can be seen to the north. The sound of water running along a stream can be heard
in the distance.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
The bridge above is covering some of the sky here, and occasionally sounds of
someone walking there are audible. The stream is clear and chilly here, formed
from meltwater that is carried from the distant mountains in the east, to meet
with the Bruinen, far to the west. Flatlands border the stream to the south,
where the bridge connects to the ground some feet above the stream.
name: Under a Covered Bridge

desc:
A bridge has been constructed to the north crossing the river in a narrow spot.
The ground here is waterlogged, with several sizeable pools of standing water
that have become quite stagnant. The air has a musty, unwholesome smell and the
environment has become an ideal breeding ground for mosquitoes and other small
insects.
name: On the Bank of a River

desc:
The bridge underfoot is composed of logs and bundles of branches lashed
together. Dirt has been rubbed into the cracks to form a sort of clay helping
insulate against the water. The hides of various animals have been strung
together above the bridge. They form a tunnel of some sort. Although stretched
as tight as possible, they sag at the midpoint between supporting poles which
have been driven into the bridge.
name: Covered Bridge over Loseithel

desc:
Planks of wood and all manner of other types of foliage have been tightly bound
together to create this bridge. A canopy of animal pelts sewn together is
stretched overhead supported only by stakes periodically set along the path.
name: Covered Bridge over Loseithel

desc:
There is a substantial drop-off to the south where the bank of the stream has
collapsed, perhaps adding risk to any attempt to travel in that direction. The
surrounding lands to the north are low and contain little in the way of
vegetation except for an abundance of coarse grass that covers the ground.
name: Hilly Bank of a River

desc:
The plains are covered with small patches of dried-out, brown grass. Some
thickets of thorny bushes lie scattered around in the fields. The ground is
hard, leaving any form of agriculture out of the question. An ancient, broken
road lies to the north and west, slowly curving through the sloping plains.
name: Barren Plain at the Road

desc:
The hills here are composed mainly of red dirt, broken stone, rough scrub brush
and sparse grass. To the north lies an ancient road. Passage west seems to be
impossible, adding to the somewhat dismal nature of the landscape. To the east
once more lie plains, while to the south are yet more hills.
name: Hills near a Road

desc:
On these arid plains nothing but the hardiest grass can survive. The bleak land
stretches out far to the south and west as a broad shelf, hardly wavering in its
depth or height. This same shelf prevents passage south. To the north an ancient
road cuts its path east and west across the shelf, bordered on the north by a
low ridge.
name: Plains near a Road

desc:
Small thickets of tangling thorn bush are dotted across the dry brown grass in
these billowing fields. The ground is hard and infertile, composed of clay and
limestone. A road can be spotted in the north, crossing the wide plains that
dominate the landscape. Far to the east the white peaks of a mountain range
reach their jagged fingers towards the sky, creating a magnificent backdrop for
the rugged looking camp located in the plains to the northeast.
name: Billowing Fields near a Road

desc:
Dry brown grass sprouts out of the red-brown earth which composes the billowing
fields rolling down from the southeast. Far apart small thickets of tangled
thorn bush give little shelter from the elements or a prying eye. In the north
more plains stretch towards the ancient, broken road, and far off on the eastern
horizon the peaks of a mountain range reach toward the sky.
name: Bleak Plains

desc:
A breach in the ancient stone wall that surrounds the camp has been hastily
patched and filled in quite recently. Until it is made more secure it is likely
that additional guards might be posted in this area. The stench of death fills
the air, suggesting that whatever battle that broke the wall happened recently.
name: Breach in a Wall

desc:
This path breaking south off the Ancient, Broken Road heads to a settlement of
some sort. Further south the trail fades into a plain, and some buildings and
tents might be seen suggesting that there may be a more permanent occupation of
this area than might be expected.
name: Path to the Bandit Camp

desc:
The slick rocky floor is framed to the north and west by smooth walls of granite
and quartz. On the walls, small clumps of white mushrooms sprout in several
cramped nooks, and large patches of green moss have taken root in some damp
soil. The floor here is very smooth, and almost featureless, as if carved out by
eons of natural waterflow.
name: Cramped Crawlspace

desc:
This cramped space, formed by dirt and grey rock, extends north into the black
of the mountain. The walls are mostly smooth, with few nooks or imperfections. A
few tiny beads of water have collected at the tip of a tiny stalactite, forming
a constant, slow drip onto the slick rock below.
name: Crawlspace

desc:
A series of wide caverns runs east and west. The stone walls show evidence of
toolwork, crudely carved where the caverns narrow, possibly to make passage
through here possible at all. The cavern floors are dusty and dry, with
footprints in the dirt.
name: Wide Cavern

desc:
This rather long cavern has a wide gap in the ceiling exposing it to the open
sky. A mossy pool of water has collected in the middle of it. To one side is a
crudely fashioned wooden rampart. Scattered in a loose u-shape around the
fortification are large stones to form an extra obstacle, perhaps.
name: Goblin Lookout

desc:
This wide, grey cavern is rather undistinctive. The walls are reasonably smooth
and loose rocks are scattered about here and there. A crudely carved stairway is
chipped into one of the cavern walls, leading up to a large stone which may be
rolled aside.
name: Empty Cavern

desc:
This chamber looks like it was carved out of the rock, the walls are too even to
be natural. An assortment of weaponry and armour has been put here, hanging from
pegs, sitting in weapon racks, or squeezed into cabinets. It is, in short, an
armoury. Judging from the quality of the equipment found here, it is not
maintained by someone who is too keen on quality and keeping things in good
condition.
name: Small Armoury

desc:
The series of caverns running from the west comes to an end then splits off into
a pair of tunnels branching north and south. The northern tunnel slopes
downward, while the southern route terminates in a winding spiral stair. A
simple guardpost seems to have been set up at this junction, with simple
furniture and an empty rack attached to a wall.
name: Cavern Crossing

desc:
A long procession of roughly hewn stairs winds up and down; hundreds of steps
lost in darkness. The stairs are crudely tooled but also made smooth by time and
evident travel. These caverns have been inhabited by something or another for
many long centuries.
name: Spiral Stairwell

desc:
Wet slime slowly ooze down the walls, gathering in shallow pools on the cavern's
floor. The small cave, little more than a recess in the rock, stinks of decaying
plant life. The air is still, thick, and humid. The ceiling is high and the
source of the watery muck is hidden in the gloom above.
name: Wet Recess

desc:
A spiralling staircase leads upwards into a round vertical tunnel, with no end
in sight. The cavern around the staircase looks like it has been expanded, the
walls show signs of being crudely hewn to widen them. Both the stairs and the
floor of the cavern are worn smooth.
name: Spiral Stairs

desc:
A wide tunnel slopes downward, winding back and forth as it descends to the
south. The tunnel is easily broad enough for several goblins to walk side by
side, and the floor and walls of the tunnel show signs of crude chiseling as if
it has been widened on purpose.
name: Sloping Tunnel

desc:
A foul odour fills the air here. The moist cavern walls are coated with a slimy
grey-green fungus, clearly the source of the malodorous stench. Green slime
drips from the cavern ceiling and walls; the muddy floor is wet and slick.
name: Dank Cavern

desc:
A pillar of rock, as broad as three full-grown trolls, dominates the centre of
this nearly circular chamber. Fungal growth covers the pillar entirely, lending
it a brown-green hue. The air is motionless, chill, and oppressively damp.
name: Dank Cavern

desc:
Three tunnels, each oddly distinct, intersect at a round chamber. To the east,
the passage is lined with phosphorescent moss so that the apparently smooth rock
beneath cannot be seen. In contrast, greenish-brown growth obscures a twisting
tunnel to the north. Southward, however, the walls are dry and appear almost
polished, as if delved by hand rather than natural in origin.
name: Dank Cavern

desc:
A thin layer of slimy mildew covers the walls and floor of this tunnel, which
slopes downward from the east and narrows abruptly into an impassable crack to
the west. Water oozes slowly from the ceiling and down the walls. The air is
cold and damp, and the footing is slick and treacherous.
name: Slimy Dead-End

desc:
A smooth, polished tunnel leading in from the north suddenly changes directions
as it meets a natural opening in the rock running westward. All signs of
construction halt at this corner, as if the delvers' plans were ruined in
likewise abrupt manner. Faint, steady warmth wafts from further down the tunnel
to the west.
name: Tunnel Elbow

desc:
This broad chamber along an east-west tunnel is apparently used by goblins for
training in combat. A small arena surrounded by stakes strung with leather
thongs lies north of the central passageway, whereas several archery targets are
set up to the south. Bloodstains and scattered bones reveal that restraint is
not always a part of goblin combat exercises.
name: Combat Chamber

desc:
A large tunnel running east-to-west broadens as it branches into a barracks to
the north and smithy to the south. Goblin fletchers have taken up residence in
this part of the cave, evidenced by bone arrowheads and wooden shafts, and
animal sinew lying along the southern wall. Loose bundles of arrows and crude
bows rest at the entrance of the barracks, ready to be taken up at a moment's
notice.
name: Fletcher's Chamber

desc:
All available space in this low, broad chamber is devoted to bedding of various
sorts. Furs, crude mats, and piles of moss lie strewn haphazardly, yet no spot
of the rock floor beneath is visible. The floor space has apparently proven
insufficient, as several hammocks hang from pegs in the walls and ceiling. Faint
warmth and the scent of smoke wafts from the south, a possible reason for the
overstuffed popularity of these sleeping quarters.
name: Cramped Barracks

desc:
A tunnel entering from the east slopes suddenly upward to pass through a narrow
crack no more than a few feet wide. Crude stairs have been carved into the rock
at irregular intervals, sufficiently shallow for goblin-sized legs. The natural
bottleneck of this tunnel affords a strong defensive position. Arms and
supplies, including many crude bows, line the walls.
name: Watchpost

desc:
Ash and soot blacken the walls of a low, broad chamber. Weapons and armour in
various states of construction and repair are strewn about the room. Along the
southern wall, a gentle depression holds hot coals for a smithy's work. A
natural chimney extends upward through the rock, making for a convenient egress
for the smithy's fumes, revealing uncharacteristic ingenuity on the part of the
goblins that use this chamber.
name: Sooty Smithy

desc:
This rocky chamber resembles a funnel, with steeply sloping sides converging
toward a crude staircase that descends eastward from its bottom. The ceiling
dips downward following the shape of the floor below. An oppressively dark
tunnel winds away from the southern entrance to the chamber.
name: Sloping Passage

desc:
A tunnel with pitch-black walls bends around a protruding obsidian rock
formation along its north-eastern wall. The obsidian is run through with grey
streaks of some unknown impurity. Scratches along the otherwise smooth stone
indicate where some creature attempted, but then gave up on liberating this
stone from the rocky walls.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
A narrow tunnel bends as it runs beneath a ceiling of hard, smooth obsidian. A
failed mine that would have run north cuts off suddenly beneath a cascade of
rubble. Several other attempts to extract minerals from the walls are also
evident along the tunnel, these more quickly abandoned.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
This tunnel makes a sharp and sudden bend from east to north. The sheer
blackness of the walls would make this abrupt turn a rather unpleasant surprise
for any who would carelessly dash along this passageway. Sundry supplies spill
out of a deep recess in the southern wall.
name: Sharp Bend

desc:
Nourished by the meltwater of the Misty Mountains towering up steeply to the
east, green grass overtakes the hard rock of the slopes. The grassland rolls
ever up and down, each decline giving a little shelter against the elements. All
over the small tracks of wildlife run irregularly, suddenly ending or starting,
sometimes with a tiny hole in the ground.
name: Rolling Plains below the mountains

desc:
The ground becomes harder and higher as it draws closer to the mountains in the
east, making it hard for the grass to grow. Some tougher thorn bushes and shrubs
are scattered among the fading grass, seemingly having little trouble surviving
in this harsh hill environment. A small stream twists through the lands to the
west.
name: Hill at the Edge of the Plains

desc:
With a swamp just to the west, this field is flat, damp and a bit smelly at
times. Low lying areas have wetland plants growing in it, and the grass rising
from the red soil is thicker than in other parts of the region. Even the wiry
brush seems healthier and more robust. Still, it is an area that offers little
of interest or even value. To the north some hills rise breaking the view, while
a river flows to the northwest.
name: Dank Field

desc:
A hill covered in rough brush and grass rise overlooking the river and plains.
Passage south and west is blocked by the steep river banks. To the north more
bleak plains as are typical of the region can be seen, while heading south
offers even more of the same kind of plains.
name: Hills by a River

desc:
Having reached the vast plains of this flat land, the stream flows at a more
languid pace in its journey to the northwest. The stream bends westwards, where
vast plains spread out as far as the eye can see. To the south, a swamp forms a
border where water and plains meet.
name: Meandering Stream

desc:
Slowly the stream wends its way through the wilderness making a lazy bend here
to the north. On its banks grows a rough green grass, mixed with small thorn
bushes. To the east rise the foothills of the Misty Mountains and to the north
and west an expanse of rolling grasslands spreads out. Flowing water has eroded
the banks to the south, blocking all travel in that direction.
name: Bend in a Stream

desc:
At this southern end of the river, plains turn to swamp, offering a haven for
all sorts of creatures who love marshy areas. To the east and west lie more
plains, while the river flows north and west for some distance. Looking
northeast, one can see several hills breaking the monotony of the landscape.
name: Swampy Plain

desc:
The ground becomes harder and rougher as it draws closer to the mountains in the
east, making it hard for the dry, brown grass to grow. But some tougher thorn
bushes and shrubs are scattered among the fading grass, seemingly having little
trouble surviving in the harsh environment. A small stream makes its way through
the landscape to the north, cutting the river bank in such a way as to block
passage. To the south lie hills, while swamps form to the east, and plains
continue far to the west.
name: Dried Grassland

desc:
Small patches of bare earth mix in with the occasional stone in these sparsely
vegetated grasslands. The ground is hard and infertile, composed of red-brown
clay and limestone. Far to the east a few foothills can be seen, the lower
slopes of the Misty Mountains.
name: Barren Grassland

desc:
These bleak plains come rolling down from the south to the north. There is close
to no vegetation at all here, save the dry, brown grass that grows in patches in
the dusty, red-brown soil. The fields extend ever onwards, and far off to the
east the ever-white peaks of the Misty Mountains reach up towards the sky.
name: Folded Grassland

desc:
The rugged crags rears above the plains below as they climb toward the mighty
peaks of the Misty Mountains. The mountain's teeth rise above the sky, gnawing
at the firmament. Brush and scrub paint the landscape in green and grey.
name: Rising Crags

desc:
These craggy hills are but the foothills of the high peaks looming above the
land to the east and south. Dangerous cracks and nooks in the rock are hidden by
shrubs clinging to every little crack and patch of earth. The land descends to
the west and north; towards numerous lower hills to the west and a
barren-looking grassland to the north.
name: Crags of the Misty Mountains

desc:
The rolling hills are strewn with large clumps of stinging nettle leaves. Some
care must be taken while crossing the hills to avoid brushing up against the
spiny bushes. To the north, fields of grass and brush cover the land between
these hills and a distant river.
name: Hills of Nettles

desc:
Patches of gorse and brush dot the faded landscape, blots of colour amidst an
otherwise colourless land. To the north are featureless plains, and to the south
are hills dusted with brush and trees. Here and there is the scuttle of small
creatures.
name: Gorse-bush covered Hills

desc:
A few thickets of tangled thorn bushes are scattered amid the small patches of
dry, brown grass that cover the plains. The landscape rolls up and down, with a
few large boulders giving scant shelter from the elements. Far to the east a
mountain range reaches up towards the sky.
name: Rolling Wasteland

desc:
Dry, brown grass covers the plains in small patches, with a few thickets of
tangled thorn bushes in between. The landscape rolls up and down, giving little
shelter from the elements. Close to the east a thickets of wiring brush can be
seen, while in all other directions, the vista is dominated by vast fields of
scraggly grass.
name: Folded Wasteland

desc:
These bleak plains all but define featureless. Flat and without any redeeming
qualities, they are an uninterrupted flat area of scrub brush, grass and brown
dirt. To the east, marshes meet the plains, and a twisted combination of mud,
logs washed from the river further east, and dense brush create an area that
cannot be crossed by even the sturdiest dwarf or strongest troll.
name: Fields Bordering a Swamp

desc:
Small thickets of tangled thorn bushes dot the dry, brown grass in these
billowing fields. The ground is hard and infertile, composed of clay and
limestone. To the east lies a swamp, while all other directions show more of the
same, tiresome plains that offer no relief from the elements.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
Here, the river meets the plains and turns them into a swampy mess. Grass and
water loving brush rise above the waterlogged soil, making passage more
difficult. To the west the swamp gives way to plains, while the swamp continues
to the south, and the river continues to flow in a southeasterly direction.
name: Swamp by Some Plains

desc:
Following the altitude of the grassland the stream takes on a mainly northwest
direction, twisting in between small ridges. The vegetation on the banks is
mainly composed of short grass, reed and a few flowers. South and west of here
swamps form along the edge of the river.
name: Twisting Stream

desc:
Cutting its path through the grassland, the stream now turns in a northwesterly
direction, following the contours of the land. The impassable banks are
overgrown with reeds, grass and small flowers. Looking east and north one can
see foothills to the Misty Mountains forming, but in the southwest grassy fields
continue, seemingly forever.
name: Stream through Some Grasslands

desc:
Plains meet river and turn into swampy marshes here. To the north and east lie
more river. To the west are more swamps, with plains and hills visible further
west. Passage south is blocked by dense vegetation and breaks in the terrain.
name: Murky Marsh

desc:
The low plains have been turned into a marsh by the surrounding waterlogged
lands to the north and east. Higher plains to the west and south remain dry, but
cannot be reached through the muck and mire of this area. Here and there swarms
of mosquitoes flit about looking for some unfortunate creature to feed from and
harass.
name: Marshy Field

desc:
These bleak plains continue far to the north and south as well to the east and
west. There is close to no vegetation at all here, save a few patches of dried
brown grass. Small, dried-out rivulets tell of a time long ago when the land was
less parched, but now dusty, red-brown soil dominates the landscape. Far to the
north a road can be seen.
name: Blasted Field

desc:
Dry, brown grass covers the plains in small patches, with a few thickets of
tangled thorn bushes scattered between them. The landscape rolls up and down,
giving little shelter from the elements. Far to the east great white peaks jut
upward into the sky, a spectacular backdrop to the otherwise repetitive fields
that extend all around.
name: Arid Wasteland

desc:
The arid fields stretch out before you, undulating gently as they progress from
north to south. A few tangled thorn bushes can be seen, a contrast to the
never-ending dry brown grass and hard limestone that otherwise dominate the
landscape. Small old dried-out rivulets tell of a time long before, but now the
dusty red-brown soil dominates the area.
name: Billowing Fields

desc:
All bold colour has departed from this land. Paints of grey and sallow yellow
wash the scrub with faded dreams. Still, a few white blooms toss in the breeze,
as the Misty Mountains loom above on the eastern horizon, stretching in an
unbroken line from northeast to southeast, its mighty peaks shrouded in clouds.
name: Faded Heath

desc:
Grey and green dominate the landscape with patches of mottled brush. To the west
the brush clears, and to the east the brush continues, albeit a different hue.
The mighty mountain range known as the Misty Mountains rises to the east,
dominating the view.
name: Grey Heath

desc:
The rolling grey wastelands begin to rise to the west, where a slender goat
trail winds up onto a high ridge that dominates the western skyline. To the
south are more wastes, and to the east, much the same, but spotted with grey
brush and clumps of wiry grass.
name: Barren Wasteland

desc:
Rising high above the surroundings, this ridge wends its way from the northeast
to the south, becoming a high saddleback. Off to the west can be glimpsed a
winding, broken road, grey and forbidding. Barren wastelands lie to the east.
Beyond, a shimmering blue-grey river cleaves the blasted lands.
name: High Ridge

desc:
A ring of hemlocks springs from the brush, their thick green spines filling the
sky like a crown. Amidst the break in the scrub, a strange mushroom fairy ring
mimics the brush. West and east the hemlocks pop amidst the scrub, their small
white flowers in contrast with the drab greens surrounding them.
name: Hemlock Grove

desc:
The low hills are lightly covered with hemlock plants and sparse brush. The
small white flowers of the hemlock plants stand in contrast to the faded greens
of the other plantlife. The great Misty Mountains rise high above to the south
like crowns against the sky.
name: Hemlock-covered Foothills

desc:
The brush here is coloured in blueish-green, thickly clumped, and studded with
nettles. A small cul-de-sac has been created by the dense foliage and towering
peaks to the south. Little shows from the sky, the brush forms a dense hedge
that covers any light from entering.
name: Thick Bluebrush

desc:
These bleak fields roll in a north and south direction, and are covered with
dense, stunted brush that struggle to rise from the barely fertile soil. On top
of a small elevation, a single gnarled tree fights to rise above the unforgiving
ground. From time to time, cold and harsh winds blow down from the mountains
rising far to the east. Badly broken ground prevents any travel south, limiting
options for the weary traveller, while foothills break the landscape closer to
the east.
name: Bleak Fields

desc:
Dry brown grass covers the hill in small patches, protruding through the thin,
red-brown soil. A few thickets of tangled thorn bushes can be spotted in this
unsheltered land. The soil is unfruitful which explains the barren view. Close
to the east lie flatter brush-covered plains. Passage west and south appear to
be impossible.
name: Barren Hill

desc:
Scattered brush and thorny bushes litter the hardened red soil. Fragments of
quartzite are the only things that can be seen jutting up from the rolling hill,
and no shelter is to be seen for miles around. Further off to the east rolling
fields stretches out. Passage to the west is blocked by harsh terrain that
offers no exit. To the south, even more hills rise.
name: Brush Covered Hill

desc:
Miles of featureless, flat terrain spread all around. Here and there grass and
scrub brush manage to find life on the nearly waterless plains. To the far east,
hills can be seen breaking the monotony of the landscape. Dense, interlocking
thickets of brush prevent all travel to the north and east, making this a rather
unpleasant place to be cornered in.
name: Brushy Plains

desc:
Long stretches of empty, grey plains spread out to the north and south
punctuated only by spurts of dark bushes and patches of hardy grass. In the
west, the land rises to a faint trail that leads higher up.
name: Barren Wasteland

desc:
A dusty trail rolls up and down this high ridgeline. Among the rocks and dust
some sprigs of hardy grass maintain a precarious existence in defiance of the
elements. Looking west, a steep incline rises along the horizon. To the east, a
small trail snakes up from dull, grey wastelands below.
name: High Saddleback

desc:
A faint goat trail winds its way from the high ridge to the north down into a
stony vale. Tough, grey-green vegetation clings to the hillsides in patches and
clumps as steep rockfaces prevent movement except along the winding trail.
name: Craggy Hills

desc:
A small valley nestles below the northern craggy hills. Flat, round stones are
scattered across the vale in a variety of shapes and sizes. Tufts of scrubgrass
and nettles poke up between the stones determined to survive the hostile
environment. To the west, a wide plain opens up while beyond, a broad ridge of
hills rises in the distance.
name: Vale of Stones

desc:
The grasses that grow here are shorter than usual, clinging to the ground in
abundance. This keeps them from blowing away and traps their own moisture
securely to the ground. The plains continue to the west and south, while a
wilted and dried field can be found north.
name: Flatlands

desc:
Dry, wilted grasses lie scattered about here. This once thriving field is no
longer fed by water and, as a result, the grasses are desiccated, and dead or
dying. The fallow fields continue onwards north and west. To the south, short
stout grasses, hardier than those found here, grow in abundance.
name: Dry Fields

desc:
This could have once been a thriving field, with healthy plant life and maybe
even produced some crops for hardworking farmers. Now it is only slightly more
than a dry desert. The long wide cracks in the ground harbour dust, dirt and
dead weeds. Little else exists here since it is so dry. The desolate fields
continue to the north, west, and south.
name: Fallow Wasteland

desc:
The ground is hard and infertile, mostly composed of clay and limestone. A thick
layer of dust covers the ground of these arid fields. A ridge ascends to the
south and east, but it allows passage to south. No other landmarks can be seen
across the wide plains, except for the large mountain range that looms in the
distance to the east.
name: Arid Fields

desc:
Grey and grey and grey. Barren and blasted, these areas are not a place any wise
man might tread. The ground is uneven and the dry earth is cracked in places and
covered with rocky formations in other. To the east, the lands flatten out more
except for a tall rocky formation.
name: Barren Wasteland

desc:
This rocky formation is butting up against the rising Misty Mountains to the
east, but further ascent is blocked by sheer walls of rough stone. The ground
has numerous cracks and crevices, some of which looks to be used as nests by
birds. Westwards, a more gentle slope descends to lower ground.
name: Atop a Rocky Formation

desc:
This small cottage has not seen any use in many years. The walls look close to
collapse, all the mortar between the stones having crumbled away long ago. The
roof is filled with large holes that allow rain and snow to fall inside. The
floor boards are rough and mouldy, creaking and groaning when the least weight
is applied on them. Old broken furniture litters the floor, and spider webs
cling all over. In a corner stands an old open stove.
name: Dilapidated Cottage

desc:
The cavern has been used as a storage room once upon a time. Now it is a mouldy
place filled with garbage. The cavern extends north into a damp cave, and a
small ladder leads up towards the ceiling. A few roots can be seen reaching
through the walls for water, and the western wall is intertwined with spidery
webs.
name: Cavern

desc:
Dug from the moist earth this damp cave has been deserted for ages. The walls,
floor and ceiling are all very rough, evidence that the cave was constructed in
haste. The sodden earth gathers enough water to create small rivulets that make
their way slowly down the walls, trickling towards the floor to gather in small,
murky, brown puddles.
name: Damp Cave

desc:
The walls, ceiling and floor are all comprised of a thick sticky mud. Rough
shoring seems to have been hastily put in but it doesn't look to be very
trustworthy. The tunnel descends deeper into the mirk to the south, while
upwards traces of limestone lead on into the dark. The incessant dripping of
water can be faintly heard percolating through the tunnel.
name: Wet Tunnel

desc:
The ground has become more watery sludge than mud, squelching and sucking
underfoot. Some boards of old wood seem to have been thrown down here to help
give some stable footing but they are now rotten and almost entirely consumed by
the mud. There is no hint of fresh air here, only stale dankess.
name: Down a Wet Tunnel

desc:
Worms and what appear to be fish bones can be seen sticking through the mud
walls, the rotten wooden scaffold holding the tunnel in place seems entirely
dilapidated and ready to give way at any moment. Water trickles down the walls
and through the ceiling, dribbling into the pool of water covering the floor.
Wading through the slosh causes considerable amounts of movement and noise, yet
the air hangs heavy and motionless.
name: Down a Wet Tunnel

desc:
The ground here rises sloping upwards and descends sharply towards the north. An
oozing stream of thick mud continuously seems to be sliding down the tunnel to
the north like a mirky honey dripping down a tree. The dripping of water
somewhere is defeaning in the dead silence of this slimy passage.
name: Steep Wet Tunnel

desc:
Up above a breath of fresh air can be felt wafting in, mixing in with the stale
air of this muddy tunnel. Down Below the tunnel dips steeply leading to darkness
with nothing but the sound of dripping water ahead. Up above an incline made of
mud, tufts of grass, and roots leads to an opening one might be able to crawl
through.
name: Muddy Cave

desc:
This outhouse has clearly seen better days -and better users. Now broken down
and reeking of unspeakable filth, it is both the only shelter from the sun for
possible miles around, but also the only place to get a little private relief if
truly needed. There is no real way of telling who might be desperate enough, or
perhaps foolhardy enough to use this building. A stinking pit opens down from
the middle of the building, and a well-worn, creaky door appears to be latchable
for a little privacy.
name: Dilapitated Outhouse

desc:
This foul, stinking pit is full of water, faeces, and other disgusting items
perhaps half a yard deep. The reeking smell seems to rise to the heavens and
there is no real relief from it. Streaks of greenish brown slime line the
crudely dug earthen walls, and the only way back up seems to require getting
covered in that same slime.
name: Stinking Pit

desc:
Piles of stone and building materials lie piled up here, ready to be used to
repair the ancient buildings and walls that make up this camp, and even perhaps
to build new structures and walls. Prisoners captured along the abandoned road
might be seen toiling away for their captors until ransomed or no longer of use
to the bandits.
name: Storage Yard

desc:
More than anything it seems the bandits hate the goblin hordes that at times
must infest the areas around the abandoned road. Since the trolls of the area
aren't eating enough of them, the cruel bandits must do their part to keep the
goblin population in check. Whenever they capture goblins alive, they are
brought here to be tortured for information and pleasure, and then killed
without mercy. Rows of severed heads serve as proof of their prowess at this
task.
name: Execution Grounds

desc:
The path heads further south into what looks like a settlement. It is possible
though this is an unsavoury place for unsavoury people, as it is unlikely that
any honest sort would live in this area. Certainly whoever lives here is bound
to be of a rather sturdy sort given the isolation and dangers surrounding them.
name: Bandit Camp

desc:
Just south of the trail entering the camp, the ground gives way to somewhat bare
and churned out ground. In rainy weather it can be quite muddy, or equally dusty
and unpleasant in warmer months. To the east is the patched up stone wall of an
ancient building, while a crude wooden wall blocks passage west.
name: Churned up Ground

desc:
Firmly inside the bandit camp, there are more open spaces that perhaps in some
ancient time were used for drilling or markets or livestock before this small
patch of buildings was abandoned and fell into ruin. To the east is another
repaired stone building with a crudely patched up door, while in all other
directions are more open areas. Perhaps the better for fighting and chasing
enemies...
name: Inside a Camp

desc:
An open field with quickly repaired stone buildings to the west serves as a
makeshift training area for the men who reside in this camp. Targets of various
sorts are placed with the building walls serving as a backstop. The ground
itself is kept mostly clean and level, and a couple of places look like they are
used to practice wrestling or other hand-to-hand combat arts. A crude barricade
to the south blocks travel, while open fields lie to the north and east.
name: Training Grounds

desc:
This flat and open field is regularly used, and so the grass and brush are
trampled by the feet of men and hooves of horses. It may serve as a marshalling
yard, or simply a place to gather and do such things as these rough, evil men
like to do in their spare time. The occasional scent of alcohol or pipe-weed
wafts through the air. To the east, a wooden barricade blocks travel.
name: In a Camp

desc:
To the east of this field lies a broken looking, yet durable stone wall that
cannot be readily climbed over. The rest of the area seems taken up by the same
wiry grass and coarse shrubs that dominate this part of Arda. Perhaps the
occupants of this camp have no ready use for this area, or perhaps it will
someday fill with tents and buildings if the camp grows.
name: Empty Field in a Camp

desc:
To the west, come the smells associated with a camp kitchen. Here in this field
lie the detritus of that kitchen- a pile of compost in one corner, a pit of
trash and bones in another, with a garden area making up much of the rest of
this location. It seems even violent, evil men know they have to eat and eat
well. To the south is a repaired stone wall of another building complex, and a
wooden barricade to the north. To the east is another open area with a grim
purpose.
name: In Front of a Kitchen

desc:
A large open fireplace heats the room and whatever happens to be in the kettle
placed over it. Elsewhere, sacks of vegetables and grains, and various other
provisions fill the room, evidence of good looting of passing travellers and
caravans. To the east lies an open area, while to the north and south, doors
offer passage to other rooms in the stone-walled complex.
name: Kitchen

desc:
Tucked away behind the kitchen, this room is filled with crate, sacks, chests,
bales and barrels of likely looted and captured goods, along with supplies
needed to run the camp. It may even be possible that more valuable goods are
hidden away out of sight...
name: Storeroom

desc:
Windowless stone walls and thick thatch roofing make up this room which is
filled with bedding and storage chests. Clearly a place for the lower status
guards and occupants of the camp to sleep, it is a cheerless room rank with the
smell of sweat, smoke and stale wine.
name: Barracks

desc:
The stone walls of this room are surprisingly clean, and the room itself is
rather well lit. Racks of weapons line two walls, while another has strange
alchemical bottles and scrolls. The faint smell of beasts, leather and oiled
steel permeate the room, and the overall orderly appearance suggests this room
is regularly used by a disciplined person or persons.
name: Armoury

desc:
This old stone building has been hastily repaired by semi-skilled hands. Gaps in
old mortar have been filled, and breaks in the wall quickly, but strongly
repaired. The dirt floor has been covered with fresh reeds from the nearby
river, and the roof given a new thatching that looks like it probably will keep
water out. To the north is a somewhat private room that might be sleeping
quarters for an important person. A desk and a couple of chairs fill out this
room, while an old wooden door patched up with hide is to the west. A simple
fireplace burns with a bright light and offers heat for cooking and warmth in
cold weather.
name: Old Stone Building

desc:
Inside this windowless room is a large bed, a nightstand with a pitcher, and a
rack for armour or weapons. Somewhat barren, it still manages to suggest that
whoever sleeps here is comfortable. A carpet made of stitched together hides
covers much of the floor, while the stone walls and thatched roof are in a
similar condition as that of the room directly to the south.
name: Leader's Bedroom

desc:
This ramshackle old hut has somehow barely stood the forces of time, time, war
and weather. It serves as a place to mostly get out of the elements, and to
access a strangely well secured cellar below. Now and then noises might be heard
from below, as if someone or something was imprisoned below, although for who's
safety is anyone's guess...
name: Decrepit Hut

desc:
One might be forgiven for at first thinking this hole was a place where foul
trolls or nasty goblins made a home, for it is both completely dark, and
disgusting with smells of filth and excrement. However, it is instead proof of
man's darker nature, and serves as a prison for some unfortunate wretch cast
down in here for reasons unknown.
name: Black Hole

desc:
Picket pins and hitching posts dot the landscape, indicating this area is used
by the camp's inhabitants to keep their mounts. A whiff of the ground and the
large amounts of horse droppings suggest why this area is muddy even in summer.
name: Muddy Corral

desc:
A dry streambed winds westward as it descends a rugged slope. To the west, steep
ravines and jagged fissures cut into the coarse, darkened rock that comprises
the surrounding mountainous landscape. Southward, sheer rockfaces thrust
ever-upwards towards rugged crags and sharp, broken peaks of various heights.
name: Rocky Incline

desc:
Towards the south, the jagged outline of a natural, rocky maw blends into the
lighter landscape beyond. Darkness progressively replaces light in the depths of
the cave to the north, and only dim outlines of dark, glistening rock can be
seen. The faint drip of liquid lightly echoes across the wide walls and ceiling
of this place, the roof of which disappears into total darkness high above. The
only suggestion of a ceiling existing at all is the tips of rocky teeth hanging
down far enough to barely discern them against the darkness.
name: Cavernous Maw

desc:
Towards the south, the darkness appears to be slightly less dense, as the
occasional small point of light can be seen, suggesting an opening in the
passages far in that direction. The hard, rocky floor of this cave abruptly
slopes downwards to the north, where darkness overtakes any last reflections of
light off the jagged stone walls.
name: Sloping Passage

desc:
The rocky passage continues to the east and west, offering little to no
visibility save where artificial light is offered. However, as if reluctant to
challenge the engulfing darkness, illumination seems to fall short of where the
distance would shine otherwise. Faint drops of water faintly echo across the
uneven walls of rock, which seem to resonate slightly louder to the east.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
A small, grubby room is pontooned to the side of the bridge. The floor is a bit
unsteady as it rises and falls in accordance with the river's mood. Mud has been
repeatedly tramped in here, and the wood planks in the walls are largely warped
and discoloured.
name: Guardroom

desc:
This cramped den extends back five or so yards before terminating. Roots fall
from the ceiling of the den and bits of fur can be seen strewn across the
ground. Something large has made its home here and bits of bedding made up of
leaves, grass, and bark have been carefully placed at the end of the den.
name: Dark Den

desc:
Hot and musty, this kitchen is dominated by a large pile of rotting meat that
has been stacked haphazardly in one corner. What seems to be a meal has been
prepared over a large pot that absolutely reeks of rot. Various cooking vessels
and tools hang from a low overhead ceiling. A couple of flickering torches
lights the kitchen, if just barely.
name: Goblin Kitchens

desc:
Dingy and dim, this corner of the cavern is cluttered with a few cabinets and
storage lockers lining the walls. Piles of unwashed wooden food bowls are
haphazardly stacked, waiting to be cleaned. A large wooden tub full of nasty
water sits nearby.
name: Cluttered Pantry

desc:
A crudely chiselled tunnel winds back and forth while rising to the south. Broad
enough for several goblins to march side by side, the chipped walls are adorned
with flickering torches interspersed with skulls. To the north, it almost smells
like food is being prepared.
name: Sloping Tunnel

desc:
Here the cold, jagged tunnels of mountainous rock branch east and west, with the
exception of a nearby rocky ascent upwards. The hard stone walls glisten with a
faint hint of moisture when artificial light is present, which the darkness
readily swallows once removed. In all directions the visibility yields to the
engulfing darkness after only a few feet, as if the light itself knows the
futility of further effort.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
A natural recess extends deep into the wall of a larger tunnel to the north.
This seems to have been considered a convenient location for tossing supplies of
all sorts. Shabby furs, short spears, and poorly maintained armour feature
heavily, with neither rhyme nor reason apparent in their placement in many
sordid piles.
name: Disorganized Alcove

desc:
Darkness besieges this cave, making it hard to distinguish anything in here. The
floor is littered with old hardened cocoons, and strands of large, sticky spider
webs. In a corner lies a pile of dried out flesh and the bones of an
unidentifiable humanoid. The air is still, filled with the scent of death and
decay.
name: Spider Cave
